rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — write 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:90 "The driver opens Cowork on the SharePoint workshop folder root"; line:100 "one organiser opens Cowork on the SharePoint workshop folder root"; line:125 "start a new Cowork session on the same workshop folder root"; line:142 "start a new Cowork session on the same workshop folder root"
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cowork's correct UI verb is 'Click New task and select <path> as the working folder' (per reference_cowork_capabilities.md 2026-04-28); 'opens Cowork on' and 'start a new Cowork session' use Code-style vocabulary in a Cowork-only exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork vocabulary: unit of work is 'task', not 'session'.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:32 "another Cowork session can read a file"; line:47 "another Cowork session can read a file"
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Inside the CLAUDE.md content block (lines 30-33) and body prose, 'Cowork session' should read 'Cowork task' to match verified Cowork vocabulary (reference_cowork_capabilities.md §Vocabulary, verified 2026-04-28).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: CLAUDE.md describes the runtime, not just the task.
Cowork is sandboxed to its connected working directories.
Cowork capability surface — task names set at New-task creation.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in. Under 20 words.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:142 "After Phase 3 creates one group synthesis file in `shared/`, start a new Cowork session on the same workshop folder root and ask Claude to create two more fake group syntheses." (31 words)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Trim to a semantic summary of what the prompt does, under 20 words — e.g. 'Ask Claude to generate two more fake group synthesis files from the rehearsal pattern.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt 1 (line 106) references 'the rollout-notes.md file you just created in the first-name folder from the participant rehearsal' — same-session scrollback is valid, and the path is genuinely variable (organiser's own name choice); no deterministic path is constructible. Mark as TODO for mechanical runner coverage.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
Preamble before action — structural trigger: 4+ named sub-steps require a preamble suppressor or body primer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt 1 (lines 106-121): 5 action items (Use/Create/Keep/Make/After saving). Prompt 2 (lines 146-158): 5 action items (Scan/Read/Use as pattern/Make different/After saving). Neither fence contains 'No preamble.' and neither has a body primer immediately after the fenced block.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble.' as the last line of each prompt fence, or add a body primer sentence after each fence: 'Claude will narrate before acting; skip past the opening and look for the listed files.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strong-bold prompt label is exactly **Prompt** — custom labels break the renderer.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Participants copy the workbook prompts into Cowork
OneDrive may need a moment before another Cowork session can read a file that was just saved by a different session
start a new Cowork session on the same workshop folder root
opens Cowork on the SharePoint workshop folder root
